def: s.f. = S يوك यूक yūka, vulg. yūk, s.m. A louse (see jūṅ).

ety: rt. yuj
def: part. (f. -ā), Joined, united, connected, attached; combined; identified (with); — prepared, arranged, adapted, fitted; fit, suitable, proper, right, just, due; — appointed; engaged, occupied (with), absorbed (in); — accompanied, furnished (with), endowed (with), endued, imbued, possessed (of), impressed (in these senses yukt is used as a suffix)
s.f. = S يکتتو युक्तत्व yuktatva, s.m. Application, use, employment (i.q. yukti); suitableness, fitness, propriety.
s.m. = S يکتتا युक्तता yuktatā, s.f. Application, use, employment (i.q. yukti); suitableness, fitness, propriety.

s.f. Joining, junction, union, connection, combination; — application, use, employment, practice, usage; appliance, means, plan, project, scheme, expedient; trick, contrivance, device, stratagem; — suitableness, adaptedness, adjustment; fitness, aptness, propriety, correctness; skill, dexterity, ingenuity, art; policy
subs: argument, right reason, demonstration, inference, induction, deduction; (in Rhet.) supplying an ellipsis

vulg. yukt-mān, adj. (f. -matī), Joined, united, tied; possessing fitness; full of contrivances or projects or plans; fertile in expedients; ingenious, clever, inventive, able, competent; — furnished with arguments; based on argument; proved, demonstrated.

ety: v.n. fr. يمن 'to be fortunate and happy'
def: s.m. Felicity, prosperity; good luck; — a token for good.
s.m. (dialec.) = yāṇ, q.v.

ety: for एउँ = Ap. Prk. एव or एम्व = S. इयत् (Vedic ईवत्)
def: adv. Thus, in this wise, in this manner; — just so, for no particular reason; without just ground, vainly, idly, causelessly, gratuitously; to please oneself
subs: adv. In this case; — generally speaking
adv. One way or other; somehow or other; — in no wise
yoṅ-hī, or yūṅ-hīṅ, yoṅ-hīṅ, adv. In this very manner, exactly thus; — without any apparent cause or reason, causelessly, &c. (i.q. yūṅ, q.v.); by chance, accidently; — cursorily, easily
adv. Be it so then; just as you please.

def: prop. n. Ionia; Greece (so called till subdued by the Romans; since then the term Rūm has been applied to the Eastern Roman empire at large).

ety: rel. n. fr. yūnān
def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Yūnān; Ionian, Grecian, Greek; — an Ionian, a Grecian; — s.f. The Greek language
subs: s.f. The Greek system of medicine.

def: adj. = yūnānī, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of Yūnānī), The Greeks.
s.m. Jonah (the prophet).

ety: S. यूनी
def: s.f. = yuvatī, q.v.
s.m. See the pop. form جوپ jūp.

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.m. Europe
s.f. Invasion; assault, attack, storm
subs: To make an attack (on), to invade, &c.

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.m. Europe ( = yūrap).
adj. European.
s.m. See the pop. form جوس jūs.
s.m. The patriarch Joseph; — a proper name.

ety: rt. yu
def: part. (used as a suff.), Attached (to); united, or connected (with), joined (to); in contact (with); accompanied, attended (by); with; possessed (of), furnished (with), endowed (with).
s.m. corr. of يوتهہ yūth, q.v.

ety: S. यूथ+कः
def: s.m. = yūth, q.v.
s.m. A flock, herd, drove (of birds, animals, &c.), a multitude, number; a body, troop, band, company (see jūth)
subs: vulg. yūthap, or yūth-pati, or yūth-nāth, s.m. Keeper or protector of a troop; lord or captain of a band; leader or head of a flock or herd; — a large elephant (the leader of a wild herd)

def: s.m. See s.v. yūtha or yūth.

ety: S. यूथिका
def: s.f. A kind of jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum (see jūhī, or jūʼī, the pop. forms).
s.m. (dialec.) = jūʼā, 'yoke,' q.v.

ety: base yuvan
def: adj. & s.m. (f. yuvati, or yuvatī), adj. Young, youthful, juvenile; arrived at puberty, adult, adolescent; — strong, vigorous, healthy; — one who has attained the age of puberty, a young man, a youth (from 16 to 17 years of age; — see the pop. form juvā)
subs: s.m., and yuva-rājya, s.m. See s.v. juvā.

ety: base of yuvā
def: adj. = yuvā, q.v. (cf. jawān).

ety: yuvā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. The state or condition of puberty, youth (regarded as a state or condition, or as a time of life), youthfulness, adolescence.
s.m. See juva-rāj, s.v. juva.
s.f. Youthfulness, youth, adolescence.

ety: = yuvā, q.v.
def: s.f. A young woman (from 16 to 30 years of age), a damsel (see juvatī).

ety: S. युवति+इनी
def: s.f. = يوتي yuvatī, q.v.
s.m. = yuvatā, q.v.
s.m. A hunting-leopard (syn. ćītā); — a small species of panther.
contrac. of zād or zāda, q.v. (used in comp., e.g. mīr-zā or mirzā, q.v.).
s.m.? Civet (the perfume so called).

ety: Parsī hizvãn; Pehl. huzvān; Zend hizva; S. जिव्हा
def: s.f. The tongue (syn. jībh); tongue, speech, language, dialect (syn. bolī)
subs: s.f. Eloquence, fluency, volubility of tongue
To let the tongue wag, to chatter, prattle
v.n. To be speechless, be unable to speak; to be stricken dumb; — to have lockjaw
s.f. Written testimony, deposition, affidavit (in a court of law)
v.t. To take down a deposition
(-kī), To obtain the assent, or concurrence, or approval (of)
(-kī), To be on the tongue (of), be talked about, be public; to be on the tip of (one's) tongue, to have or know (a thing) by heart
v.t 'To put on the tongue,' to taste
(-ko), To utter, say, speak of, mention
(-kī), lit. 'To lay hold of the tongue' (of); to prevent (one) from speaking; to interrupt; to seize on the utterances (of), to criticise, to cavil (at)
or zabān phernā, To retract, to eat one's words; to equivocate, to prevaricate, to shuffle
(-kī), The tongue to be exercised in correct articulation; to be able to articulate or pronounce correctly, to speak with correct articulation
(apnī), To talk much, talk at a great rate; to use foul language, to give abuse
To live by a glib use of the tongue, or by flattery
To speak with bated breath; to detract in whispers
adj. & s.m. Skilled in language; — one well-versed in a language; a poet; a scholar; a linguist
s.f. Knowledge of a language, linguistic attainments
adj. 'Long-tongued'; loquacious; speaking much and malevolently; impudent, saucy; abusive, scurrilous
(-ko), To show the tongue (to a physician)
(-meṅ), To interfere by speech, to inquire, ask, request
adj. Talked about, spoken of, reported, public; current, used, in use
(-ke), To be talked about, be currently reported, be often mentioned; to become current (as speech), to be generally used (by), be commonly employed (by), to pass into an idiom
To be careful as to what one says, to speak carefully or cautiously
(apnī), To control (one's) tongue, to be careful as to how one speaks or what one says; to hold one's tongue
v.t. To give utterance to, to utter, pronounce, speak
(-kī), To escape the lips (of); to be uttered, be said

(-kī), To interrupt the speech (of); to make a clicking noise with the (apnī) tongue expressive of regret at a slip or error; to be sorry for what one has said; to grieve, lament; to speak foolishly or inconsiderately
or zabān-ko muṅh-meṅ rakhnā, To keep the tongue under control; to hold one's tongue, to be silent
(apnī), To give loose to the tongue; to speak out
To smack the lips (over), to enjoy or relish; to enjoy dainties
(apnī), To wag the tongue, to speak; to ask, request.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زبان (zabān, zubān), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *hijwáH, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ȷ́iȷ́ʰwáH. First attested in c. 1603 as Middle Hindi زبان (zban /⁠zabān⁠/),. Doublet of جِیبھ (jībh).

def: s.m. Tongue (of fire, or of a buckle, &c.), flame (of a candle).

ety: of, -kī
def: adv. By word, orally, &c.

ety: az+bar, 'upon,' qq.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Above, upper, high, superior; greater, larger; — the Persian term for the short vowel َ a (so called because it is placed over a letter; — the A. syn. is fatḥa)
subs: adj. Having the upper hand, superior, victorious; vigorous, powerful, strong, athletic; high-handed, overbearing, tyrannical, harsh, oppressive, violent
s.f. Superiority; powerfulness; high-handedness, oppression, tyranny, violence, force; — adv. By violence or force, forcibly, violently, wrongfully, unjustly, arbitrarily, wantonly
(-se, or -par), To use violence or force (with or towards), to force, compel; to oppress
v.n. To be superior, to have the upper hand.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَبَر (zabar).

ety: zabar, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Violence, oppression.

ety: zabar, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Higher, upper; superior.
s.m. A kind of emerald, a chrysolite; a topaz; a beryl; jasper.

ety: act. part. n. of ذبح 'to cut, cleave, slaughter'
def: s.m. Slaughterer, sacrificer; butcher
subs: The two stars α and β in the left horn of Capricornus; the twenty-second mansion of the moon.

ety: inf. n. of ذبح 'to slaughter,' &c.
def: s.f. Slaughtering, cutting the throat of, slaying, sacrificing; — ẕibḥ, vulg. ẕibaḥ, s.f. An animal prepared for slaughter, or sacrifice; a sacrifice, a slaughter
subs: To slaughter, cut the throat of (an animal intended for food, agreeably to the Moḥammadan law); to sacrifice, to immolate; to slay, kill, execute, put to death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذِبْح (zibh), from Arabic ذَبْح (ḏabḥ).

ety: for A. z̤abyuṉ
def: s.m. The gazelle, the Arabian antelope; a deer, a chevril.

ety: v.n. fr. ذبح; and = maẕbūḥ
def: part. adj. Slaughtered, sacrificed; — s.m. An animal that is destined or prepared for sacrifice, an intended victim, a sacrifice ( = ẕabīḥa)
subs: A surname of Ishmael (who, according to the generality of Moḥammadans, was the son whom Abraham designed to sacrifice, and not Isaac).

ety: for A. ذبيحة ẕabīḥat, fem. of ẕabīḥ
def: s.m. An intended victim, a sacrifice ( = ẕabīḥ); an animal lawfully slaughtered (and fit for food).

(act. part. of ضبط 'to keep, preserve,' &c.), Careful, cautious, provident, prudent; practising self-control, self-denying, abstemious, continent; — exact, correct, accurate, strict; punctual; — restrainer; possessor, master, ruler, governor
subs: Lord or ruler of all; the Omnipotent.

ety: for A. ضابطة, fem. of ẓābit̤, q.v.
def: s.m. Universal rule, general canon, established practice; rule, canon, law, ordinance, regulation; custom; judicial usage; manner of proceeding, procedure
subs: (-kā), To employ legal means, take legal measures; to act according to law
or ḥasb-ě-ẓābit̤a, adv. According to rule or established practice; through the regular channel; in due course
s.m. A body of instructions for revenue officers
adj. and adv. Regular, formal, en règle; — according to rule or established practice; through the regular channel; in due course, duly
adj. Irregular, contrary to rule, informal, unusual.

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Being according to law or rule; conformity to law or rule, regularity

ety: inf. n. of ضبط 'to keep, preserve'; 'to withhold, restrain,' &c.
def: s.m. Keeping, taking care of, guarding, defending, watching over, ruling, governing; regulation, government, direction, discipline; restraint, control, check; taking possession of, possession, seizure, confiscation, sequestration, resumption
subs: v.t. To preserve, watch over, &c.; to rule over; to restrain, control, check; to take possession of, to seize, attach, confiscate, &c.
v.n. To be restrained, be kept under control; — to be taken possession of, be seized, or confiscated, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَبْط (zabt), borrowed from Arabic ضَبْط (ḍabṭ).

def: s.f. Control, restraint; seizure, confiscation, &c. ( = ẓabt̤, q.v.); — adj. Seized, confiscated, &c.
subs: v.n. To come under confiscation, to be seized, or confiscated, &c. ( = ẓabt̤ honā)
adj. Deserving of confiscation; liable to seizure or confiscation; contraband.

ety: v.n. fr. زبن; a post-classical word, with amplification of meaning in Persian
def: adj. Weak, infirm, helpless; vile, evil, ill, bad, wicked, faulty; unfortunate, unlucky; unhealthy (air or climate).
s.f. Infirmity, weakness, helplessness; vileness, badness, ill, faultiness, wickeduess, vice, depravity; infamy, disgrace.

ety: v.n. fr. زبر 'to write'

s.f. Child-birth (see jaćgī).

def: s.f. A woman who has recently brought forth (see jaćā).

subs: s.f. Beating and thumping; assault and battery; fight, conflict (syn. mār-pīṭ).

def: part. & s.m. (f. zādī), Born, born of; — son, offspring (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ādam-zād, ādmī-zād; parī-zād; shāh-zāda or shah-zāda, shāh-zādī or shah-zādī, &c., qq.v.s.v. ādam, &c.).

r: ẓād, ẓwād, ẓād-ě-mǒʻjama, ẓād-ě-manqūt̤a, z, d
def: (called ẓād-ě-mǒʻjama, or ẓād-ě-manqūt̤a), is the twenty-first letter of the Urdū alphabet (the fifteenth of the Arabic, from which it is taken); — it occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic and the secondary formations therefrom. In Hindūstānī (and Persian) it is pronounced like ز z; but in Arabic it is a strongly articulated palatal d. As a numeral it denotes 800.

ety: perf. part. of zadan; Pehl. zat; Zend jata, rt. jan; S. हत, rt. हन्
def: part. Struck, stricken, smitten, beaten; affected, afflicted; oppressed; — accentuated; drawn up in line, arrayed (e.g. ṣaf-zada); trimmed, adorned (used in comp., e.g. āfat-zāda, q.v.s.v. āfat); — old, worn out, ragged (as clothes, &c.).

ety: perf. part. of zādan; Pehl. zāt; Zend zāta, rt. zan; S. जात, rt. जन्
def: part. & s.m. (f. zādī), Born, born of; — son, offspring (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ādam-zād, ādmī-zād; parī-zād; shāh-zāda or shah-zāda, shāh-zādī or shah-zādī, &c., qq.v.s.v. ādam, &c.).

def: part. adj. Perishing, becoming lost or destroyed; lost, wasted, fruitless, abortive
subs: v.t. To cause, or allow, to perish, to destroy; to lose; to consume, waste; to apply (labour, &c.) to no purpose, to misapply; to frustrate
v.n. To perish; to pass away, to die; to be or become lost; to be wasted.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَایِع (zāyi'), borrowed from Arabic ضَائِع (ḍāʔiʕ), from ضَاعَ (ḍāʕa). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi ضایع (ẓay'). Compare Punjabi ضائَع (ẓāyaʻ) / ਜ਼ਾਇਆ (zāiā).

ety: zāʼe, fr. zādan, rt. Zend zan = S. जन्+dim. aff. ća = S. क

ety: v.n. of ذأف 'to die quickly'
def: s.m. Quickness of death; sudden death.

def: s.m. (f.?) Victory, triumph; success; profit, gain
subs: adj. Having the signs of victory, victorious
s.m. A long, sharp, iron prong; a dirk, stiletto
s.m. A congratulatory letter on the occasion of a victory
adj. & s.m. Victorious, triumphant; — a conqueror.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظفر (zafar), from Arabic ظَفَر (ẓafar).

def: s.f. Saffron (cf. Ital. zafferano).

def: adj. Of saffron; saffron-coloured, yellow; — s.f. Saffron-colour, a yellow colour.

ety: prob. a corr. of A. صفير, inf. n. of صفر 'to whistle'; cf. P. sapīl
def: s.f. Whistling; a loud whistle (such as is used in making pigeons fly)
s.f. A whistle.

ety: = S. काक; H. काग

s.m. = زاج zāj, q.v.
s.m. = زاج zāj, q.v.
s.m. = زغال zag̠āl, q.v.

ety: contrac. of next, q.v.
def: s.f. A kite; — a spring, &c. ( = zag̠and).

def: s.f. Jump, leap, spring, bound; sally; flight, levity
subs: (-par), To jump, leap, bound, to spring (upon), pounce (upon).

ety: inf. n. of ذهب 'to go, depart,' &c.
def: s.m. Going, departure; failing, failure (as of the heart), loss (as of reason)
s.m. Gold; — the yolk of an egg.

ety: act. part. of زهد 'to abstain from'
def: adj. & s.m. Abstinent, religious, devout; — one who shuns the world and exercises himself in acts of devotion, a devotee, a monk, recluse, hermit; a zealot.

ety: zāhid, q.v.+āna = Zend aff. ana or āna = S. अन

ety: zāhid, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Abstinence, continence, devotion; the life of a devotee or recluse.
(act. part. of ضحك 'to laugh'), Laugher (at), mocker, ridiculer, satirist.

ety: act. part. n. of ذهل 'to forget, neglect,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Forgetful, neglectful, negligent, careless, inattentive; — a negligent or inattentive person, &c.

ety: part. n. fr. ذهن; see ẕěhn
def: adj. Intelligent, possessed of understanding, sagacious, acute, clever, skilful, ingenious; endowed with a retentive mind (syn. ẕakī).
adj. Reduced (by sickness), thin; — sad, melancholy, low-spirited.

ety: act. part. of ظہر 'to appear,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Outward, exterior, external, extrinsic, exoteric; appearing, apparent, overt, open, perceptible, visible, perceived, plain, evident, manifest, conspicuous, ostensible; — the outside, the external appearance; the external, or outward, or extrinsic state, or condition, or circumstances (of, -kā); the outward, or apparent, character, or disposition of the mind
subs: s.m. One who looks only to the exterior
adj. & s.m. Minding the outside or exterior, &c.; — a superficial observer; one who is influenced by outward show; one who takes things for what they seem
s.m. The head or chief of the caste of ḥalāl-ḵẖor, q.v.
adj. Specious, plausible, showy, ostentatious; pretentious; ceremonious, formal
s.f. Speciousness; show, ostentation; ceremony, formality; — z̤āhir-dārī baratnā (-kī), To make a show (of), to affect, pretend, to assume; to pass off (for)
v.t. To make evident or plain (to, -se), to show, exhibit, manifest, tell, explain, disclose, discover, reveal; to publish; to expose; — to make a show of, to make a pretence of, to pretend, affect
adv. Openly; apparently, in public; in appearance, ostensibly
s.m. Outward appearance and inward qualities; — adv. Outwardly and inwardly; inside and out

or ho-jānā, v.n. To be or become apparent or visible; to become manifest (from, -se), to be clearly shown (by), to appear, to become public, to become evident or known (to, -par), to be disclosed, be divulged, be revealed.

ety: v.n. fr. iii of ظہر
def: s.m. Aider, assistant, associate, ally; — a poetical name of a Persian poet of Faryāb.
= P ظاهره z̤āhira (for A. ظاهرة, fem. of z̤āhir), adj. f. = z̤āhir, q.v.
= H ظاهرا z̤āhirā (for A. ظاهرة, fem. of z̤āhir), adj. f. = z̤āhir, q.v.

def: adv. Outwardly, openly, publicly, manifestly, evidently, plainly, apparently, to all appearance, seemingly, ostensibly.
adj. External, apparent, &c. ( = z̤āhir, q.v.).

ety: inf. n. of ذهل 'to forget; to neglect,' &c.
def: s.m. Forgetting, neglecting, leaving careleesly, abandoning.

ety: for A. زحمة, inf. n. of زحم 'to press, as in a crowd,' &c.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَحْمَت (zahmat), borrowed from Arabic زَحْمَة (zaḥma), from زَحَمَ (zaḥama).

ety: rel. n. fr. zaḥmat, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Disquieted, in pain, troubled, afflicted; — one who is afflicted or troubled, &c.

ety: Zend gara; S. गर
def: s.m. Poison, venom, virus; (met.) anything bitter or disagreeable; gall and worm-wood
subs: lit. 'To vomit poison'; to say venomous, spiteful, or bitter things; to speak ill (of), to calumniate; to sneer (at)
and zahr-āmez, adj. Mixed with poison, poisoned, envenomed, venomous
s.m. lit. 'Poison-air'; a dangerous disease of horses and elephants in which the yard and testicles of the animal become suddenly swollen and violently inflamed

v.n. To swallow or take poison (on account of, or through love, &c., for anyone, -kisī-par); to be exceedingly envious or jealous (of anyone, -kisī-par); to imitate an act unsuitable to one's state or condition, to ape
v.t. To give (one) poison, to poison
(-ko), To be regarded as poison, to be hateful (to)
v.n. To swallow as poison, to do (a thing) by force, or with great repugnance
s.m. Bezoar, an antidote to poison

ety: v.n. fr. ظہر 'to be or become outward or exterior,' &c.

ety: of azhar, rt. زهر 'to shine,' &c.

ety: fr. zahr, q.v.

r: ẕāl, dāl-ě-mǒʻjama, th, thou, ze, z., ẕuʼl-ḥijja, abjad
def: called dāl-ě-mǒʻjama, is the thirteenth letter of the Hindūstānī or Urdū alphabet, and ninth of the Arabic (from which it is taken). It is pronounced by the Arabs like th in the English thou; but in Urdū its sound is not to be distinguished from that of ze, which is exactly our own letter z. It is used, by way of abbreviation, for ẕuʼl-ḥijja, the last month of the Arabic lunar year; and in reckoning by abjad, it stands for 700.

ety: zang, q.v.+Zend aff. ar, S. ara, or ra
def: s.m. Verdigris; rust; (in comp.) acetate; sulphate, &c.

ety: zangār, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Rusty; rust-coloured; — colour of verdigris, green.
s.m. See s.v. zang.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنگبار (zaṅgibār). By surface analysis, زَن٘گ (zaṅg) + بار (bār).

ety: zang, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. & s.m. Of Ethiopia, Ethiop, Moor, negro; black; — an Ethiopian; a negro, a blackamoor

ety: zang, 'bell,' q.v.+dim. aff. ūla or ula = S. ल or र
def: s.m. A little bell; small globular bells hung round the rim of a tambourine; a cymbal.

ety: see zan, fr. zadan
def: s.f. Beating, striking, attacking, &c. (used in comp., e.g. lat-zanī, 'kicking'; rah-zanī, 'highway robbery').

ety: for A. زانٍ zāniṉ, act. part. of زني 'to commit adultery'
def: s.m. Adulterer, fornicator, whoremonger.

ety: for A. زانية, fem. of zānī
def: s.f. An adulteress, a strumpet, harlot.

ety: S. शृङ्गवेर 'horn-shaped'; Gr. ζιγγίβερις; Lat. zingiber
def: s.f. Dry ginger.

ety: zan, q.v.+A. jalab, fr. جلب 'to drive,' &c.
def: s.m. A pander to his own wife, a cuckold.
s.f. Panderism to one's own wife, cuckoldom.

ety: = S. शृङ्खला
def: s.f. Chain; fetters (zanjīr, 'chain,' is applied to elephants, as rās, 'rein,' is applied to horses)
subs: s.f. A chain for the neck in which stones are set at intervals.

def: s.m. A necklace; — chain-stitch.
adj. & s.m. Chained, in chains; mad, insane; — a prisoner; — a maniac.

ety: zan, q.v.+ka = S. क
def: s.f. A little woman.

ety: prob. for P. zanka, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Effeminate; weak, impotent; — a eunuch; — a dancing-boy.

ety: inf. n. of ظنّ 'to think, opine,' &c.
def: s.m. Thought, opinion, notion, idea, supposition, conjecture; suspicion, evil opinion; jealousy
s.m. = sannāṭā, q.v.
(rel. n. fr. z̤ann), Thought, supposed; suspected.

ety: Pehl. shnuk, zānuk; Zend zhnu; S. जानु
def: s.m. The knee; the lap
subs: v.n. To change the knees, to rest the knees alternately (in kneeling)
To sit on the hams, to kneel.

ety: fr. Zend zar = S. हृ (हृणीयते; and, in some senses, app. = S. हृ, हर्); cf. āzār
def: s.m. Groan, plaint, lamentation, wailing (cf. zārī); desire, wish; collection, multitude, crowd; (as a suffix) place where anything grows in abundance, place, bed, garden ( = stān or istān; e.g. gul-zār, 'a rose-garden') — adj. Groaning, lamenting, afflicted; thin, lean, weak
subs: and zār-qitār, s.m. Great lamentation; — adv. With great lamentation; piteously, bitterly, exceedingly, to excess (e.g. zār-zār ronā, 'to weep bitterly')
s.m. Great lamentation; — adj. Thin, lean, emaciated; weak, feeble.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زار (zār), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *jāráyati.

ety: Zend zairi, rt. zar = S. हरि 'yellow'; but cf. Zend zaranya, 'gold'; and S. हिरण्य्
def: s.m. Gold; money, riches, wealth
subs: s.m. A principal sum; pure gold
s.m. Income, revenue; receipts, proceeds, profits
part. adj. Covered or plated with gold, gilded, gilt
s.m. Brocade, gold tissue, cloth of gold ( = zar-bāft); — one who works in gold thread, a gold-lace worker
adj. Woven in gold, brocaded, embroidered
s.m. Remaining money, balance, outstanding balance
adj. & s.m. Venal, mercenary; selfish, sordid; — a mammonist; a sordid or selfish person
adj. Purchased with one's own money, purchased (as a slave)
adj. Producing gold; rich (a country); fertile
v.t. To enrich; to make productive, to fertilize
adj. & s.m. Wealthy, rich; — a moneyed man
s.f. Embroidery; — zar-dost, adj. & s.m. Fond of money, covetous; — one who is fond of money, a miser
s.m. 'Red gold,' pure gold; gold; gold coin; — name of a suit in cards (see s.v. tāj)
s.m. Silver; — a suit in cards (see tāj)
s.m. Security for money-payment, a deposit-security
s.m. Amount of a debt; debts
s.m. 'Gold shoe'; embroidered shoe; (met.) a douceur, a bribe
s.f. Beating gold into thin leaves; the business of a gold-beater

s.f. The business of a goldsmith; — a secret or enigmatical mode of speech (similar to what, in English, is called the p language, the letter z being used instead of p; as tuzum kazhāṅ jazātize huzo, i.e. tum kahāṅ jāte ho)
s.m. The yellow stamina of a rose
adj. Gilded, gilt; inlaid with gold
s.m. Money to be got in, balance to be received, debts due, claims, demands, outstandings.

ety: inf. n. of زرع 'to sow'
def: s.m. = zirāʻat, q.v.
= P زراتشت zarātusht, prop. n. = زردشت zardusht, q.v.

ety: for A. ظرافة, inf. n. of ظرف 'to be clever, or ingenious, or polite,' &c.
def: s.f. Beauty; ingenuity, &c. ( = z̤arf, q.v.); wit, humour, jocularity, facetiousness, pleasantry, fun, jest.

ety: for A. ظرافةً, acc. of z̤arāfat
def: adv. Facetiously, in fun, by way of jest.

ety: v.n. fr. ضرّ 'to harm,' &c.
def: s.m. Harm, injury, hurt, mischief, damage; defect, deficiency, detriment, loss; — poverty; affliction, distress, anguish
subs: (-ko), To cause harm to come (to), to harm, hurt, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضرر (zarar), from Arabic ضَرَر (ḍarar).

def: = P زرادشت zarādusht, prop. n. = زردشت zardusht, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of ضرب 'to beat, strike,' &c.
def: s.f. Beating, striking; — stamping; sealing; coining (money); a beating, blow, stroke (of a whip, &c.), wound (of a sword); a sprain, hurt, injury; loss, damage; — stamp, impression (on a coin, &c.); violence, vehemence, emphasis; (in Arith.) multiplying, multiplication; — making or constituting (a saying) proverbial; — a piece (as of cannon, &c.; e.g. ćahār ẓarb top, 'four pieces of cannon'; cf. rās, and zanjīr); — adj. & s.f. Beaten, struck; stamped, coined, &c.; — coined money, a coin
subs: v.n. To suffer injury, or damage, or loss
(-ko), To sustain an injury, to get hurt, &c.
(-ko), To stamp, coin; — to multiply (one number into, -meṅ, another)
v.t. To multiply ( = ẓarb denā, q.v.)
v.t. To strike, give (one) a blow; to hammer; — to stamp or impress (upon, -meṅ)
or ẓarb-ě-mas̤al, adj. & s.f. Constituted, or passed into, a proverb; proverbial; current; notorious; a form of saying, a common saying, a proverb, saw
or ẓarb-ḵẖāna, s.m. 'The stamping- or coining- house, the mint.

def: s.m. = P ضربت ẓarbat, s.f. (for A. ضربة, n. of un. fr. ẓarb, q.v.), One blow, a blow, a stroke, &c.
For this and other compounds beginning with zar which are not given below, see s.v. زر zar.
s.f. = P ضربه ẓarba, s.m. (for A. ضربة, n. of un. fr. ẓarb, q.v.), One blow, a blow, a stroke, &c.
s.f. pl. (of ẓarbat), Blows; strokes, &c.

ety: Pehl. zart; Zend zairit, rt. zar; S. हरित, rt. हृ
def: adj. Yellow (syn. pīlā); pale, pallid, wan, livid
subs: s.m. 'Yellow water'; bile; serum; pus or matter running from a wound
s.m. The golden oriole, Oriolus kundu (commonly called the 'mango-bird,' because it arrives when the mango-tree blossoms)
adj. Yellow; (met.) bashful; s.m. The colour yellow
adj. & s.m. Hypocritical; malignant; — a hypocrite; a flatterer
v.n. To become pale or livid.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زرد (zard).

ety: fr. zard, q.v.
def: s.m. A sweet dish made of rice, raisins and almonds cooked in spices and coloured with saffron (a kind of pulāʼo, but without meat)
subs: (eaten with pān, or chewed alone; — cf. sūkhā); — the yolk of an egg (more commonly zardī, q.v.).

ety: zard, q.v.+aff. ak = S. क; or = zarda
def: adj. & s.f. Yellowish; — a carrot.

ety: zard, q.v.+Zend aff. ī = S. इ
def: s.f. Yellowness; yellow colour; paleness; jaundice; — yolk of an egg; — a gold mohur
subs: (-par), Paleness to come (over the face), to become pale.

ety: Pehl. zartusht; Zend zarathustra
def: prop. n. Zoroaster, founder of the religion of the Magi.

ety: inf. n. of ظرف; see z̤arāfat
def: s.m. Ingenuity, skill, cleverness; beauty, excellence; elegance (of manners), politeness ( = z̤arāfat); — capacity, capability; a receptacle, vessel, vase; (in Gram.) an adverb (of time or place); — adj. Ingenious, skilful, clever; beautiful, graceful; elegant, polite
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظَرْف (zarf), from Arabic ظَرْف (ẓarf).

ety: zar, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Anything woven with gold thread; gold lace; gold brocade
subs: s.m. A manufacture in gold thread; a gold-lace worker
s.f. The business or trade of gold-lace working

ety: zār, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Weeping, sighing, groaning, wailing, lamentation, begging and praying, supplication, entreaty, crying (or cry) for help.

r: ẕarīʻa, ẕariʻa, ẕarīʻat, ẕirāʻ, orig, wasīla, ẕarīʻa paidā karnā, ẕarīʻa rakhnā, ẕarīʻe, ẕarīʻe-se, ba-ẕarīʻa, ke, is ẕarīʻe-se
def: (for A. ذريعة ẕarīʻat, fr. ذرع 'to stretch forth the arm'; cf. ẕirāʻ), s.m. orig. 'A cover or screen (as a beast, &c.) behind which a hunter lurks and approaches game'; (hence) a means of access or approach, medium, means, instrumentality, agency, cause, occasion, interest, favour, ingratiation, intervention, intercession (syn. wasīla)
subs: To find means; to make interest
postpn., and ba-ẕarīʻa, prep., By means (of, -ke), by, by the medium (of), through the instrumentality or the intervention (of); in or by virtue (of); under cover (of)
By this means; hereby; by these presents.

ety: act. part. of ضرب 'to beat,' &c.
def: part. adj. Beating, bruising, striking; stamping, coining.

ety: v.n. fr. ظرف; see z̤arāfat
def: adj. & s.m. Clever, ingenious; elegant, polite, gallant; excellent, good; — witty, facetious, jocose, arch, comical, waggish; — a wit; a wag.

ety: z̤arīf, q.v.+āna = Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. = z̤arīf; — adv. Ingeniously, skilfully, &c.; wittily, facetiously.

ety: prob. a corr. of A. ضريح ẓarīḥ
def: s.f. (local) A railing or lattice-work surrounding a temple or tomb.

ety: Zend zairina = S. हरिण
def: adj. Golden; made of gold; — zarīna, s.m. Golden stuffs or goods
subs: s.m. Name of a bird: (met.) the sun.

ety: Zend zairina = S. हरिण
def: adj. Golden; made of gold; — zarīna, s.m. Golden stuffs or goods
subs: s.m. Name of a bird: (met.) the sun.

ety: inf. n. of ضرط
def: s.m. Breaking wind.

ety: inf. n. of زرق 'to cast (the eye
def: s.m. Turning (the eye) towards an object (so that scarce anything but the white appears); — hypocrisy; fraud, imposture; — detraction
subs: s.m. Glare, glitter, show, magnificence, pomp, splendour; glittering or shining cloth, splendid clothes; — thunder and lightning.

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).

ety: prob. fr. Zend zairina or zaranya; S. हरिण or हिरण्य
def: s.m. Arsenic, orpiment (syn. hartāl).
s.m. = ẕarra, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of ضرّ 'to harm,' &c.
def: s.m. = ẓarar, q.v.

ety: i.q. ẕarra, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), A little; — a little while, short time; a slight or trivial matter; — adv. Just, would you just, please, kindly (e.g. ẕarā yěh uṭhā-denā)
subs: adv. Little by little, by degrees; every bit, every jot and tittle
adj. (f. -ī -sī), A little; slight, trivial, trifling; — s.m. Just a little
adv. In a little while, in a very short time, soon, quickly; — with much trouble or difficulty.
ex: Close the door, if you may
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذره (zarra), from Arabic ذَرَّة (ḏarra).

ety: for A. ذرة ẕarrat, n. of un. fr. ذرّ 'to sprinkle, scatter,' &c.
def: s.m. A mote, atom, particle, a little, jot, tittle, an ace (see ẕarrā or ẕarā); — adj. (f. -ī), Little
subs: ẕarra-wār, adj. Like an atom, minute, a little; humble, lowly.

def: (intens. n. fr. ضرب 'to beat,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'One who beats or strikes much'; a stamper (of coin), a coiner; a mint-master.
s.m. pl. (of ẕarra), Motes, atoms, particles, &c.

ety: dim. of ẕarra or ẕarrā, q.v.
def: s.f. A minute particle, an atom, a very little, &c.

ety: v.n. fr. ضر 'to harm,' &c.
def: adj. & adv. Necessary, needful, requisite, expedient; urgent; unavoidable, indispensable, essential, imperative, &c.; — urgently; certainly, assuredly, of course; without fail; absolutely; peremptorily
subs: or ẓarūr-hī, or biʼẓ-ẓarūr, adv. Necessarily, of necessity, perforce; urgently; indispensably
or jā-ẓarūr, s.m. A necessary, a privy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضرور (zurūr), from Arabic ضُرُور (ḍurūr).

ety: for A. ضرورة; see ẓarūr
def: s.f. Necessity, need, want, exigency; indigence; — compulsion, constraint, force, violence
subs: (-kī), Need (of a thing) to arise, to have need (of), to have occasion (for)
adj. Necessitous, needy, in want ( = ḥājat-mand)
(-ko), To prove useful (to one) in time of need, to serve (one) in time of need.

ety: for A. ضرورةً, acc. of ẓarūrat
def: adv. By necessity, of necessity, necessarily, perforce.
adj. Necessary, &c. ( = ẓarūr, q.v.); — s.f. (local), A necessity, a thing needful ( = ẓarūrat).
(for A. ضرورية, fem. of ضروري), adj. fem. = ẓarūrī, q.v.
s.f. pl. (of ضرورية, fem. of ضروري), Necessary or indispensable things, necessaries, requisites.
